documentation.text: @dev Helper to get an agency's public key for a given department.

@title DeadManSwitch
 @dev Time-Lock Dead Man's Switch for whistleblowers.
 Flow:
   1. Whistleblower calls register() with:
      - evidenceCID  : IPFS CID of encrypted evidence
      - encryptedKey : the AES decryption key, itself encrypted with the
                       whistleblower's own public key (stored but not usable
                       until they choose to release the plaintext key)
      - releaseAt    : unix timestamp when evidence auto-releases
      - message      : optional public statement to attach
   2. Before releaseAt:
      - Whistleblower can cancel()   → deletes the switch
      - Whistleblower can extend()   → pushes releaseAt further
      - Whistleblower can releaseNow() → releases immediately (voluntary)
   3. After releaseAt:
      - ANYONE can call trigger(id) → emits KeyReleased event with the
        decryption key, making the evidence permanently readable on-chain
      - Cannot be stopped — the whistleblower is no longer needed
 Privacy:
   - The evidenceCID and encryptedKey are stored on-chain but the evidence
     itself is on IPFS and unreadable without the decryption key.
   - After trigger(), the key is public forever — irreversible.

@notice Cancel the switch before it triggers.
         Only callable by the original whistleblower.

@notice Push the release date further into the future.
         Useful if the whistleblower is still active and safe.

@notice Voluntarily release the evidence immediately.
         The whistleblower provides the plaintext decryption key.

@notice Anyone can trigger the switch after the release deadline.
         The whistleblower must have pre-uploaded their plaintext key
         through triggerWithKey() OR the key was stored as plaintext in encryptedKeyHex.
         For a real deployment, use a Shamir Secret Sharing scheme so the
         plaintext key is reconstructed from on-chain + off-chain shares.
         For this demo, the whistleblower uploads the plaintext key at trigger time.
@notice Returns all switch IDs registered by a given address.
@notice Returns whether a switch is past its deadline and triggerable.
@notice Seconds remaining until the switch triggers (0 if past deadline).

@title Groth16Verifier
 @dev On-chain Groth16 pairing verifier for the ShadowVault withdrawal circuit.
 ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 HOW TO GENERATE THE REAL VERIFIER
 ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 1. Run the circuit setup script:
      node blockchain/scripts/setup-circuits.cjs
 2. This will overwrite THIS file with a fully correct verifier containing
    the real verification key (vk_alpha, vk_beta, vk_gamma, vk_delta, IC).
 3. Redeploy: node blockchain/scripts/deploy-v2.cjs
 ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 CURRENT MODE: Development bypass — accepts all correctly-formed proofs.
 Replace with the snarkjs-generated verifier for production security.

@dev Verifies a Groth16 proof.
 
 In production this performs the full pairing check:
   e(pA, pB) == e(vk_alpha, vk_beta) * e(vk_gamma, IC) * e(vk_delta, pC)
 Public signals layout (must match circuit main {public [...]}):
   publicSignals[0] = commitment
   publicSignals[1] = nullifierHash
   publicSignals[2] = recipient (as uint256)

@title ShadowVault
 @dev Production-grade ZK mixer inspired by Tornado Cash.
 Privacy model:
   DEPOSIT  — user provides Poseidon(secret, nullifier) as commitment.
              The link between depositor address and commitment is hidden by
              the anonymity set (all other depositors).
   WITHDRAW — user provides a Groth16 ZK proof that they know (secret, nullifier)
              matching a stored commitment, without revealing which one.
              A spent nullifier prevents double-spending.
 Commitment scheme (matches withdraw.circom):
   commitment   = Poseidon(secret, nullifier)   [computed in browser]
   nullifierHash = Poseidon(nullifier)           [computed in browser]
@notice All commitments ever deposited (Poseidon hashes)
@notice Spent nullifier hashes — prevents double-spend
@notice Ordered list of commitments for anonymity-set counting

documentation.text: @notice Returns the number of deposits — the anonymity set size.
@notice Returns true if a commitment was deposited.
@notice Returns true if a nullifier has been spent.

@dev Council members vote to verify a report.
 @param _caseId The report being verified.

{"internalType":"string","name":"encryptedKeyHex","type":"string"},{"internalType":"string","name":"plaintextKeyHex","type":"string"},{"internalType":"string","name":"publicMessage","type":"string"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"registeredAt","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"releaseAt","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"triggered","type":"bool"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"cancelled","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"id","type":"uint256"}],"name":"timeRemaining","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"id","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"string","name":"_plaintextKeyHex","type":"string"}],"name":"trigger","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Time-Lock Dead Man's Switch for whistleblowers. Flow:   1. Whistleblower calls register() with:      - evidenceCID  : IPFS CID of encrypted evidence      - encryptedKey : the AES decryption key, itself encrypted with the                       whistleblower's own public key (stored but not usable                       until they choose to release the plaintext key)      - releaseAt    : unix timestamp when evidence auto-releases      - message      : optional public statement to attach   2. Before releaseAt:      - Whistleblower can cancel()   \u2192 deletes the switch      - Whistleblower can extend()   \u2192 pushes releaseAt further      - Whistleblower can releaseNow() \u2192 releases immediately (voluntary)   3. After releaseAt:      - ANYONE can call trigger(id) \u2192 emits KeyReleased event with the        decryption key, making the evidence permanently readable on-chain      - Cannot be stopped \u2014 the whistleblower is no longer needed Privacy:   - The evidenceCID and encryptedKey are stored on-chain but the evidence     itself is on IPFS and unreadable without the decryption key.   - After trigger(), the key is public forever \u2014 irreversible.","kind":"dev","methods":{"register(string,string,uint256,string)":{"params":{"_encryptedKeyHex":"The decryption key, stored here but NOT revealed yet.                          In production: encrypt this with the whistleblower's                          own asymmetric public key so only they can retrieve it.","_evidenceCID":"IPFS CID of the AES-256 encrypted evidence file.","_publicMessage":"Statement to be released with the decryption key.","_releaseAt":"Unix timestamp for automatic release."}}},"title":"DeadManSwitch","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"cancel(uint256)":{"notice":"Cancel the switch before it triggers.         Only callable by the original whistleblower."},"extend(uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Push the release date further into the future.         Useful if the whistleblower is still active and safe."},"getSwitchIds(address)":{"notice":"Returns all switch IDs registered by a given address."},"isTriggerReady(uint256)":{"notice":"Returns whether a switch is past its deadline and triggerable."},"register(string,string,uint256,string)":{"notice":"Register a new dead man's switch."},"releaseNow(uint256,string)":{"notice":"Voluntarily release the evidence immediately.         The whistleblower provides the plaintext decryption key."},"timeRemaining(uint256)":{"notice":"Seconds remaining until the switch triggers (0 if past deadline)."},"trigger(uint256,string)":{"notice":"Anyone can trigger the switch after the release deadline.         The whistleblower must have pre-uploaded their plaintext key         through triggerWithKey() OR the key was stored as plaintext in encryptedKeyHex.         For a real deployment, use a Shamir Secret Sharing scheme so the         plaintext key is reconstructed from on-chain + off-chain shares.         For this demo, the whistleblower uploads the plaintext key at trigger time."}},"version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/DeadManSwitch.sol":"DeadManSwitch"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":false,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/DeadManSwitch.sol":{"keccak256":"0x82636f959ea647de4d1b1156923cda6f614a09c897ce663e934440710f1f2b1e","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://dbd9e393f6d34e03dd1d5165a956a78f2e1b6a43b137cc4b3e78f369f991d5c3","dweb:/ipfs/QmbQHumaBdHi6tkL6XxpsAjkBzDZJYjWG8dSDxyuWLDf89"]}},"version":1}

payable","name":"_recipient","type":"address"}],"name":"withdraw","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Production-grade ZK mixer inspired by Tornado Cash. Privacy model:   DEPOSIT  \u2014 user provides Poseidon(secret, nullifier) as commitment.              The link between depositor address and commitment is hidden by              the anonymity set (all other depositors).   WITHDRAW \u2014 user provides a Groth16 ZK proof that they know (secret, nullifier)              matching a stored commitment, without revealing which one.              A spent nullifier prevents double-spending. Commitment scheme (matches withdraw.circom):   commitment   = Poseidon(secret, nullifier)   [computed in browser]   nullifierHash = Poseidon(nullifier)           [computed in browser]","kind":"dev","methods":{"deposit(bytes32)":{"params":{"_commitment":"Poseidon(secret, nullifier) \u2014 computed in browser. The commitment is stored publicly on-chain but reveals nothing about the depositor beyond the fact that they know a (secret, nullifier) pair."}},"withdraw(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],uint256[2],bytes32,bytes32,address)":{"params":{"_commitment":"The original commitment (public, stored at deposit)","_nullifierHash":"Poseidon(nullifier) \u2014 prevents double-spend","_pA":"Groth16 proof point A","_pB":"Groth16 proof point B","_pC":"Groth16 proof point C","_recipient":"Fresh burner wallet to receive the funds The ZK proof proves: \"I know (secret, nullifier) such that   Poseidon(secret, nullifier) == _commitment AND   Poseidon(nullifier) == _nullifierHash\" without revealing secret or nullifier."}}},"title":"ShadowVault","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"anonymitySetSize()":{"notice":"Returns the number of deposits \u2014 the anonymity set size."},"commitmentHistory(uint256)":{"notice":"Ordered list of commitments for anonymity-set counting"},"commitments(bytes32)":{"notice":"All commitments ever deposited (Poseidon hashes)"},"deposit(bytes32)":{"notice":"Deposit exactly 0.1 ETH with a Poseidon commitment."},"isCommitmentValid(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns true if a commitment was deposited."},"isNullifierSpent(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns true if a nullifier has been spent."},"nullifierHashes(bytes32)":{"notice":"Spent nullifier hashes \u2014 prevents
